evidence: The authors quantify feature contribution using "variable importance (Gini index)" to rank evolutionary information types as Family, Target, and PFAM (high importance) versus TS-SMART, TS-TIGR, TS-PRINT, and TS-Gene3D (low importance). They directly interpret this ranking to explain model performance differences ("probably due to the low model performance and target coverage") without permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance validation. Features are heterogeneous in type (sequence-based domains vs structure-based superfamilies) and coverage (PFAM/Family/Superfamily annotate >74% of targets; SMART/TIGRFAM/PRINT/Gene3D <50%), conditions under which Gini importance is biased upward for high-coverage features regardless of true predictive contribution. While held-out AUC test sets validate overall model performance, they do not validate the importance ranking itself.
